title: Project Report: Small Office IT Setup — IT Support / Helpdesk
author: Gagan Pasupuleti
summary: Design and document network for 15 users: IP plan, Active Directory, backup, security baseline.
source_file: CodeQuest project specification (official docs + industry patterns)

chapters.title: Project Overview
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Project Overview** *(Level: Intermediate)*

**Small Office IT Setup** is a intermediate-level end-to-end project for IT Support / Helpdesk. Duration: 2–3 weeks. Design and document network for 15 users: IP plan, Active Directory, backup, security baseline. Tools: Windows Server docs, Network tools.

Code Reference:
```text
Objective: Network diagram with IP addressing
Objective: AD user/group structure
Objective: Backup policy document
Objective: Security baseline checklist
```
What it shows: Lists measurable outcomes before you start building.

### What it actually is
A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.

### When to use it
At project kickoff.

### Where to use it
IT Support / Helpdesk portfolios and CodeQuest project submissions.

### Real use example
A candidate lists this project on a IT Support / Helpdesk resume with a demo link.
key_takeaways: A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.
A candidate lists this project on a IT Support / Helpdesk resume with a demo link.

chapters.title: Phase: Network
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: Network** *(Level: Intermediate)*

IP plan and diagram. Complete these tasks: Subnet; VLAN; Firewall rules. Deliverable for this phase: Network diagram Visio/draw.io.

Code Reference:
```text
- [ ] Subnet
- [ ] VLAN
- [ ] Firewall rules
```
What it shows: Checklist format helps track phase completion.

### What it actually is
Phase Network is one step in the full project lifecycle.

### When to use it
During week 1 of the project timeline.

### Where to use it
IT Support / Helpdesk agile sprints and coursework milestones.

### Real use example
Team demo shows the Network diagram Visio/draw.io to the teacher.
key_takeaways: Phase Network is one step in the full project lifecycle.

chapters.title: Phase: Backup
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: Backup** *(Level: Intermediate)*

3-2-1 backup policy. Complete these tasks: Schedule; Restore test. Deliverable for this phase: Backup policy PDF.

Code Reference:
```text
- [ ] Schedule
- [ ] Restore test
```
What it shows: Checklist format helps track phase completion.

### What it actually is
Phase Backup is one step in the full project lifecycle.

### When to use it
During week 3 of the project timeline.

### Where to use it
IT Support / Helpdesk agile sprints and coursework milestones.

chapters.title: Phase: Security
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: Security** *(Level: Intermediate)*

Baseline hardening. Complete these tasks: MFA; Patch policy; AV. Deliverable for this phase: Security checklist signed off.

Code Reference:
```text
- [ ] MFA
- [ ] Patch policy
- [ ] AV
```
What it shows: Checklist format helps track phase completion.

### What it actually is
Phase Security is one step in the full project lifecycle.

### When to use it
During week 4 of the project timeline.

### Where to use it
IT Support / Helpdesk agile sprints and coursework milestones.

### Real use example

chapters.title: Final Deliverables and Review
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Final Deliverables and Review** *(Level: Intermediate)*

Submit all final deliverables: Network diagram; AD design; Backup policy; Security baseline. Skills demonstrated: Networking, AD, Security. Prepare a 5-minute walkthrough video and README for CodeQuest teacher marking.

Code Reference:
```text
Deliverable: Network diagram
Deliverable: AD design
Deliverable: Backup policy
Deliverable: Security baseline
```
What it shows: Final checklist ensures nothing is missing at submission.

### What it actually is
Deliverable review confirms end-to-end project completion.

### When to use it
Before deadline and teacher review.

### Where to use it
CodeQuest project gallery and interviews.

### Real use example
A student submits Network diagram and passes the rubric.
key_takeaways: Deliverable review confirms end-to-end project completion.
